docs.title: GOAL
docs.text: This short course on the Internet of Things is designed to give a first understanding of the main issues in developing IoT solutions. We do believe the most efficient way to learn this subject is experimenting, this is way the course encourage attendants to hands-on. No specific background is needed, even if a basic knowledge of electronics and computer science might help.

docs.title: DAY 1 (3 hours)
docs.text:
• Introduction to the IoT
  • IoT and Big Data: we can quantitatively measure phenomena that up to yesterday we were only able to guess
  • A network of Resource Constrained Devices: if computational power, memory, energy, bandwidth and costs are not constrained, it is likely Internet, not Internet of Things

docs.title: DAY 2 (3 hours)
docs.text:
• The reference hardware: ESP32
  • The Maker approach: focus on effectiveness, namely doing the right things
  • Hands on with the Arduino Ide and Wokwi
  • A simple integration of sensor/actuator
  • Communicating over WiFi

docs.title: DAY 3 (3 hours)
docs.text:
• The Engineer approach: beyond effectiveness, efficiency matters, namely doing the thing right
  • The LEAN approach: build, measure, learn
  • The need of an Operating System
  • FREERTOS
  • https://docs.espressif.com/projects/esp-idf/en/latest/esp32/api-reference/system/freertos.html
  • Hands on on FREERTOS or RIOT
  • A simple integration of sensor/actuator
  • Communicating over WiFi

docs.title: DAY 4 (3 hours)
docs.text:
• The Very Large Scale IoT Testbed IoT-LAB
  • FreeRTOS on IoT-lab
  • openlab/appli/iotlab_examples/tutorial/
  • https://www.iot-lab.info/legacy/tutorials/getting-started-tutorial/index.html
  • https://iot-lab.github.io/docs/tools/consumption-monitoring/
  • A simple Project

docs.title: Tools and Materials
docs.text:
For each group * ESP32-WROOM-32 e.g. Available on Amazon * A breadboard e.g. Available on Amazon * Wires e.g. Available on Amazon * Minimal actuator, namely a led and resistors - resistor 330 Ohm e.g. Available on Amazon * Minimal analog sensor, namely a potentiometer 10 KOhm e.g. Available on Amazon * Minimal digital sensor, namely a button - resistor 1 KOhm e.g. Available on Amazon * A more interesting digital vibration sensor SW-420 e.g. Available on Amazon

  • Code and Slides
  • The Espressif software development environment
  • Arduino Ide
  • Wokwi
  • FREERTOS
  • IoT-LAB
  • Thingsboard

docs.title: Takeaway ONE
docs.text:
• IoT is fuel for Big Data Analytics

  • "When you can measure ... you know something". Lord Kelvin docet.

  • IoT provide quantitative evidences on phenomena that we used to investigate only qualitatively

docs.title: Takeaway TWO
docs.text:
• IoT is a network of resource constrained devices
  • Constraints require design choices
  • Beyond the maker approach, namely when effectiveness meets efficiency

docs.title: Let's start by simulating it
docs.text:
Wokwi is an online Electronics simulator. You can use it to simulate Arduino, ESP32, STM32, and many other popular boards, parts and sensors.

  • The simplest actuator, namely a led
  • The Simplest sensor, namely a button
  • A bit more interesting sensor, namely a potentiometer
  • A simple example with SR04 Ultrasonic Sensor
  • It's time to be connected by MQTT
mosquitto_pub -h test.mosquitto.org -t "topicName/led" -m "on"
mosquitto_pub -h test.mosquitto.org -t "topicName/led" -m "off"

NOTE: for the sake of convenience, we will use WiFi connectivity, however it should be now clear WiFi is usually not appropriate for IoT applications due to the excessive energy demand.

docs.title: It is time to Work with a real device
docs.text:
• Download the code from Wokwi. It is also available on https://github.com/andreavitaletti/IoT_short_course/tree/main/src/simulator
  • The easiest way it to use the Arduino IDE

docs.title: DAY 3
docs.text:
First of all, why do we need an Operating System on IoT devices. Here there is a nice explanation of the main features provided by FreeRTOS, namely

  • Multitasking
  • Scheduling
  • Context Switching
  • Real Time Applications
  • Real Time Scheduling

Finally a nice example of the design of a real-time application

A book on FreeRTOS.

The reference code is available

docs.title: Development environment
docs.text:
To develop our solution on our ESP32, we need to setup the environment as described here. A very convenient way is to use docker as explained here.

To further simplify the development process, a multi-platform Virtualbox image is available here. Next we will focus on this method. Credentials to work with the virtual machine are root/root and devel/devel

The reference folder for the code and the examples is https://github.com/espressif/esp-idf.git which is already availabe on the virtual machine on

  1. ssh devel@localhost -p 2222 ... the password is devel
  2. get_idf ... we set up the development environment
  3. cd ~/esp
  4. cp -r ./esp-idf/examples/get-started/hello_world/ ./workshop/ ... make a copy of a dir in the example in the workshop dir
  5. cd ./workshop/hello_world/
  6. idf.py build ... i t takes some time
  7. idf.py flash ... be sure the ESP32 is connected to \dev\ttyUSB0 and check it is visible in the virtual machine
  8. idf.py monitor ... to exit from the monitor ctrl+T ctrl+X
